name: Route annotations: schema migration + sqlc queries
description: Add the database columns and queries that back user-editable annotations on routes: a free-form note, a starred/favorite boolean, and free-form tags. Tags are many-per-route and shared across a device's tag catalog, so they live in a separate table. This feature only establishes the schema and sqlc queries; no HTTP handlers yet (that's route-annotations-api).
acceptance_criteria: New migration 009_route_annotations.up.sql adds: routes.note TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '', routes.starred BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false, and a new table route_tags (route_id BIGINT REFERENCES routes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, tag TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (tag = lower(trim(tag)) AND length(tag) BETWEEN 1 AND 32), PRIMARY KEY (route_id, tag))
009_route_annotations.down.sql reverses both changes cleanly (DROP TABLE route_tags; ALTER TABLE routes DROP COLUMN note, starred)
Partial index CREATE INDEX idx_routes_starred ON routes(dongle_id) WHERE starred = true to keep the starred-only filter fast
Index CREATE INDEX idx_route_tags_tag ON route_tags(tag) to support listing which tags exist per device (via join to routes)

sql/queries/routes.sql gains SetRouteNote, SetRouteStarred queries (both parameterized by route id); sql/queries/route_tags.sql is added with AddRouteTag, RemoveRouteTag, ListTagsForRoute, ListTagsForDevice (distinct tags across a dongle_id), and ReplaceRouteTags (one transaction: delete existing + insert new)

ListRoutesByDeviceWithCounts is updated to also return route.note, route.starred, and an aggregated tags array (ARRAY_AGG over route_tags via a LATERAL subquery) so the list endpoint can surface annotations without a second round-trip; existing callers continue to compile
sqlc generate is re-run, generated Go files are committed, go vet ./... and go test ./internal/db/... pass
Applying the up migration against a fresh comma database succeeds and can be rolled back with the down migration
notes: route_tags.route_id is declared as INT (not BIGINT as the spec text suggested) to match routes.id SERIAL and every other route-referencing table (segments, trips, events). Using BIGINT there would be a type mismatch. Up/down migration not exercised against a live DB in this session -- no running Postgres was available -- but the migration is covered by the (skipped) integration test suite in internal/db/route_annotations_test.go which replays every *.up.sql migration whenever DATABASE_URL is set.
